[[File:Rights.svg|thumb]]
Rights are a common concept in politics and one which is

== Physiological Needs ==
These correspond to the Omega Covenant

They are necessary for life and immortality

Physiological needs are basic and the

== Constructive Needs ==
Constructive needs are needs which are aspirational in their nature. We move them to a

== Deconstructive Needs ==
Deconstructive needs are needs which are enforced from society. These needs are pathological, but they are induced by society.

The important implication of deconstructive needs is that they are not wants. What is a want for one person is a deconstructive need for another.


There is a common saying “Happiness equals reality minus expectations”. This is the fundamental nature of a deconstructive need. If society or an individual imposes a kind of expectation that can limit happiness on someone then they are responsible for meeting that expectation to compensate for the lost happiness.

This is an extremely foreign perspective to Western society.

This isn’t to say all expectations can or should be met. Many expectations cannot be reasonably met such as becoming president. However many things people attribute as unreasonable expectations such as the incel expectation of sex can actually be fairly easily provided for through things like mandated therapy until they are able to effectively relate to others and form relationships.

[[Category:The Pride]]
[[Category:Ethics]]
[[sn:Four rights]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
